title: Cycle 12 dead-scope finding (TIME-CRITICAL for Twilit): S5443 has ZERO sites in named files; plan premise unexecutable as written
body:
Failure-mode capture per PDR-066: tidy-plan Cycle 12 premise mismatched to actual code surface.

## Observation

Plan `.agent/plans/agentic-engineering-enhancements/current/post-m1-attestation-tidy-up.plan.md` §Cycle 12 declares:

> Cycle 12: S5443×14 fixture replacement in
> agent-tools/tests/collaboration-state/watcher-staleness.unit.test.ts
> (10 sites) + watcher-heartbeat.unit.test.ts (4 sites) + consistency
> sweep in adjacent files.

Direct grep against the current tree (HEAD `26f8e7cb`):

```text
$ grep -rn "S5443" agent-tools/ --include="*.ts"
agent-tools/tests/collaboration-state/collaboration-state.integration.test.ts:13:// S5443 (publicly-writable directories) and signals intent at the call
$ grep -c "S5443" agent-tools/tests/collaboration-state/watcher-staleness.unit.test.ts
0
$ grep -c "S5443" agent-tools/tests/collaboration-state/watcher-heartbeat.unit.test.ts
0
```

The named files contain **zero** S5443 sites. The single S5443 occurrence in the entire agent-tools workspace is a one-line explanatory comment, not a fixture pattern. Cycle 12's substantive premise (14 fixture sites to replace) does not exist in the current code.

## Diagnosis

Three plausible causes:

1. **Stale plan author**: the author counted S5443 sites against an earlier branch state that has since been cleaned by an unrelated commit. The plan was not re-grounded against current HEAD before R3 refinement landed.
2. **Wrong identifier**: the cycle author meant a different SonarCloud rule ID or a different fixture pattern; "S5443" is a transcription error.
3. **Already-landed**: a prior cycle silently replaced the 14 sites; Cycle 12 should be marked complete.

Without artefact evidence for any of these (`git log -S "S5443"` would help; not run from this seat), the cycle as written is **un-executable** — there is no scope to replace.

## Cure

Plan amendment options for the human plan-owner / Director:

- **Option A — Verify identifier and recount**: if "S5443" is the correct identifier and the count was wrong, narrow the cycle to the single occurrence in `collaboration-state.integration.test.ts:13` and decide whether the explanatory comment is itself the fixture or whether the cycle is now vestigial.
- **Option B — Identify the intended pattern**: if S5443 was a transcription error, the plan-owner identifies the intended SonarCloud rule ID or fixture token and rewrites Cycle 12 against the current code.
- **Option C — Mark Cycle 12 as completed by prior cycle**: if the work was silently absorbed, mark Cycle 12 complete in the plan and re-target the sequencing for Cycles 13-15 to depend on Cycle 11.
- **Option D — Remove Cycle 12 entirely**: if the scope has dissolved, delete the cycle and re-thread depends_on chain `cycle-13 → cycle-11` directly.

Without owner / Director direction, I am NOT touching Cycle 12; the failure-mode capture is the deliverable.

## Pointer

- Plan file: `.agent/plans/agentic-engineering-enhancements/current/post-m1-attestation-tidy-up.plan.md` §Cycle 12 (lines ~127-136 of the YAML cycles block).
- Reviewer-CRITICAL items folded into Cycle 12: none (no CRITICAL findings against the cycle in §Reviewer R3 table; the cycle was treated as low-risk fixture-replacement).
- Behaviour-note for next plan author: re-ground numeric counts (`N sites`, `M occurrences`) against current HEAD at plan-authoring time; counts that decay between branch refactor and plan landing produce un-executable cycles.

Standing by to retarget Cycle 12 if Director / plan-owner re-shapes the cycle.

— Eclipsed Watching Secret (`00d760`)
